item.title: Learning Operations Lead (FTC)
item.description: Job description Learning Operations Lead (FTC) Contract type: 9 months FTC Location: London with a minimum of 2 days per week in the office Salary: London c£60,000per annum plus civil service pension scheme employer contribution of 28.9% The closing date for applications is 11.59pm Sunday 14th June 2026 Interview will take place at our offices in Victoria, London on 19th and 22nd June. The role is responsible for delivering all of the business operations elements that support the L&D team’s delivery of high quality, effective and engaging learning and coaching at the NAO. Reporting into the Director of Learning, Development & Talent on a day-to-day basis but with a dotted line to the team leads you will work with the Learning leadership team to ensure that learning strategy elements are delivered as effectively and efficiently as possible. You will oversee the team responsible for core administration tasks relating to deployment of programmes, but also work with the whole L&D team, and the business, to ensure that budgeting, evaluation, monitoring, data reporting and risk and governance aspects are all managed effectively. This role supervises two learning coordinators. Responsibilities Oversight of core learning programme delivery elements including management of the LXP, deployment, logistics and monitoring processes as well as advising on communications and marketing. Working with the Director of Learning, Development & Talent define the evaluation strategy and then implement and embed the agreed approach. Determine appropriate data sets including monthly scorecards, to be monitored and shared, working with fellow People Team colleagues and the business to support our learning culture. Dealing with ad hoc data requests as required, in conjunction with the administrators. Complete Operational Planning, team allocation, project creation and cash budget estimates for the year ahead Manage the day-to-day L&D budget and expenditure and conduct monthly project reviews and signoff Manage the L&D Business Intelligence data in the cube and any FF supporting activities Maintain, develop and improve the LXP, ensure pages are up to date with engaging content, create and maintain system processes, resolve or escalate technical issues, manage metadata; constantly develop, improve and review new possibilities regarding usage and efficiency. Responsible for the design and implementation of a Learning Content Register to be used consistently across the L&D function, maintaining accurate content records, review criteria and version control. External training management – business cases and payment Own and manage the L&D policies. Maintain L&D team MS Teams file sharing structure to ensure information is stored in compliance with internal data security requirements. Support the rest of the L&D team in creating best practice processes for end-to-end learning programme creation and maintain with an eye to quality and ISQM1 standards. Report on specific controls within the ISQM1 standards on an agreed regular basis. Consult with L&D specialists and other key stakeholders on design of content within the LXP to maximise functionality and reach. Oversee the risk register and governance processes. In conjunction with rest of team and L&D Leadership develop the Learning Communication strategy and plan, including Merlin pages. Manage procurement and contracts incl. SLAs incl. Civil Service Learning Transparency report / Annual report /other ext comms Oversight of L&D inbox management Develop improved processes for sourcing and managing facilitators and associates Day to day management of two team members, including mid and end of year appraisals Stay informed of industry trends related to learning technology and learning analytics to drive continuous improvements in process and our learning offer. Skills required: Technical Skills Learning Experience Platform (LXP) Management Maintaining and improving LXP functionality Managing metadata and system processes Troubleshooting and escalating technical issues Data Analysis & Reporting Creating and monitoring monthly scorecards Handling ad hoc data requests Managing L&D Business Intelligence data Budget Management Operational planning and cash budget estimation Managing day-to-day L&D budget and expenditure Digital Tools Proficiency MS Teams file structure management Familiarity with BI tools and data cubes Project & Operational Skills Operational Planning Team allocation and project creation Governance & Compliance Managing risk register and governance processes Ensuring compliance with ISQM1 standards and internal data security Policy Management Owning and updating L&D policies Soft Skills Stakeholder Engagement Consulting with L&D specialists and key stakeholders Communication & Marketing Developing learning communication strategies Advising on internal communications and marketing Problem-Solving & Continuous Improvement Identifying opportunities to improve processes and systems Doma
item.dc:creator: National Audit Office

item.title: Programme Support Officer -PCH
item.description: Programme Support Officer Partners in Care and Health Location: Westminster, London: home-based or hybrid Salary: Grade 3 - £34,434 (home-based) or £38,752 (hybrid requiring 2 office days per week) Employment Type: fixed term until 31 December 2026 with possible extension subject to confirmation of continued structure Hours Per week: 35 About the LGA The Local Government Association (LGA) is the national membership body for local authorities. We work on behalf of our member councils to support, promote and improve local government. We are a politically led, cross-party organisation that works on behalf of councils to ensure local government has a strong, credible voice with national government. We influence and set the political agenda on the issues that matter to councils to help them deliver local solutions to national problems. We also provide councils with the tools and resources to respond to challenges present and emerging, and support improvement across the sector. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of the community to this high-profile role. About PCH Partners in Care and Health (PCH) helps councils to improve the way they deliver adult social care and public health services and helps Government understand the challenges faced by the sector. The programme is a trusted network for developing and sharing best practice, developing tools and techniques, providing support and building connections. Our work is entirely funded by grants and contracts currently awarded by the Department of Health and Social Care. Our support is offered to councils and systems without charge. https://www.local.gov.uk/partners-care-and-health . About the role We are looking for a Programme Support Officer to join the Partners in Care and Health team (PCH) Programme Management Office (PMO). You will be involved in delivering our range of social care programmes to help councils and local systems support their local communities. These include: sustainable and personalised care, health and wellbeing, adult safeguarding, learning disabilities and autism, leadership, finance, digital care technology and service innovation, implementation of social care reform proposals, social care workforce planning. In this role, you will provide project and programme management support across the DHSC funded Continuous Improvement Programme. You will often be first point of contact for advice and guidance and play a significant part in the delivery of the PMO objectives and outcomes. Strong candidates will demonstrate experience of: * Programme and project management * Co-ordination of activities across a team and Programme * Risk Management * Benefit Realisation In our current situation, you should be aware that: The position is required to support immediate delivery needs The organisation is consulting on its operating model The grade and responsibilities are subject to confirmation Priority consideration may be given to employees at risk where they have the required qualifications and experience About you You will be able to work independently across multiple projects at any one time. Your work will include a range of support services all to corporate guidelines and standards including: routinely keeping project and programme documentation and records up to date, analysing spreadsheets and producing reports, taking minutes and following up on actions. Successful applicants will demonstrate the following attributes: * a team player who is flexible and capable of working with minimal supervision * well organised with the ability to plan and deliver a varied workload * ability to prioritise work, multi-tasking to deliver to deadlines * someone who can identify and resolve problems * strong communicator able to work clearly and confidently with colleagues, senior officers and stakeholders * excellent attention to detail; and * highly capable and well-motivated. To apply If you would like to be considered for the role, please complete the online application form and include a supporting statement detailing how you meet the criteria of the role before the closing date. We are specifically assessing candidates against the following: * Programme and project management experience. * Previous administrative experience and knowledge of office systems, software packages and processes, ideally gained in a programme/project-based environment * Strong organisational skills, and the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines with excellent attention to detail. * Strong analytical skills with the ability to track and monitor budgets, analysing income and expenditure activity, identifying and escalating issues * Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to deal with colleagues, members, and suppliers with courtesy, tact and sensitivity * Excellent written and oral skills, with the ability to accurately summarise and convey complex information and give clear advice on procedural issues. * Ability to work as part of a team an
item.dc:creator: Local Government Association

item.title: Head of Green Party Group Office
item.description: Head of Green Party Group Office Salary: Grade 7 - £80,360 per annum (Including London Weighting) Location : Westminster, London About us The Local Government Association (LGA) is the national voice of local government, championing councils across England and Wales. We support, promote and drive improvements so that local government can thrive. We are politically led and cross-party, giving councils a powerful voice at the heart of national decision-making. From shaping policy to delivering practical support, we help councils turn ambition into action and deliver real change for communities. The role Our six group offices – Labour, Conservative, Reform UK, Liberal Democrat, Independent and Green Party, play a vital role in supporting councillors across England and Wales. As Head of the LGA Green Party Group Office, you will shape the support for and impact of Green Party councillors at the LGA. This is a unique opportunity to be at the forefront of a growing and influential political group, helping to establish and develop their presence within the organisation. You will work closely with the LGA Green Party Group Leader, Group Executive and wider membership, ensuring councillors are informed, confident and fully equipped to make an impact across the LGA’s boards and committees. As part of the LGA’s Extended Leadership Forum, you will also help drive forward wider organisational priorities, campaigns and partnerships, working collaboratively across the political spectrum to deliver meaningful outcomes. The requirements This is a senior and influential role requiring strong political awareness including of different political cultures and values, and a deep understanding of the opportunities and challenges facing local government. You will bring: - A proven track record of leadership and delivery at a senior level - Strong, credible relationships with politicians, senior leaders and partners - Strategic thinking combined with the ability to act quickly and decisively - Confidence in providing clear, authoritative advice in a dynamic environment - Experience of business and budget planning, and leading a high-performing team Most importantly, you will be a trusted and inspiring leader, able to build confidence and influence at every level. To apply If you would like to be considered for the role, please complete the online form and include a supporting statement detailing how you meet the person specification in the JD before the closing date. Closing date: 21 June 2026 Interview date: 30 June 2026 We use anonymous recruitment, which is the practice of hiding candidates’ identities. This process involves removing any identifying information from candidates’ applications which includes names. At the LGA we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees where everyone is treated with dignity and respect. Our recruitment decisions are based on fair, open competition, with appointment on merit. ***The LGA are proud to be awarded a “Disability Confident Leader”. Please contact us via *** Recruitment@local.gov.ukif you require a reasonable adjustment, which is a change to the format or conditions of the recruitment process to accommodate any disability-related needs you may have. Our benefits LGA annual leave entitlement is 29 days, rising to 31 days after three years’ service and to 33 days after five years’ service, plus all UK public holidays (pro rata for fixed term and part time contracts).* Flexible & agile working – office-based staff are required to work in the LGA’s London office two days per week as a minimum, subject to business need and this may vary significantly between different roles and from week to week. Future development – face-to-face and virtual courses are scheduled in line with developmental goals The LGA offers 24/7 access to a free, confidential employee assistance programme managed by Health Assured. This service is also available for Dependants. Family friendly policies For more of LGA’s benefits and working with us, please view our webpage https://www.local.gov.uk/benefits-and-working-lga Our values & behaviours Our Values and Behaviours are there to give us an understanding of who we are as an organisation and who we want to be. They are a framework to help us understand how we work with each other how we work with our members and stakeholders our expectations for ourselves and each other Our values are: Collaboration – We value collaboration, to pursue shared goals by encouraging and utilising diverse perspectives and working with internal and external colleagues across the organisation. Inclusion – We value inclusion, where everyone feels valued, understood, and supported to contribute meaningfully. Ambition – We value ambition, reflecting our desire to achieve best possible outcomes for local government, communities, and individuals. Respect – We value respect, to ensure fairness, recognition, and shared accountability.
